review_title: Domo Provides Critical BI Functions at Lower Cost
description: Overall Domo provided all the functions needed out of our BI software and allowed for the connections needed and provided excellent customer service when needed.
author: Austin S.
pros: Domo provides all of the essential functions of other products including data connectors, data visualization, data ingestion and exports, as well as dashboard creation. Domo does have a learning curve to learn the difference in how the back end functions are different, but anyone with previous experience should be able to pick it up.
cons: At times Domo can be slower than other products, and does sometimes crash when ingesting large amounts of data. We also used a browser based UI which could cause trouble if their were browser issues, etc.

review_title: Work can be stressful - but DOMO can help!
description: My overall experience with DOMO is a pleasant one. I have all my main filters saved so I am able to dwindle down to my saved features and I get right to my answer. This help a lot because I work on a lot of accounts so it is tedious work. But DOMO allows me to effectively and efficiently move my workload along!
author: Joy K.
pros: I use DOMO frequently for my job to collect data metrics. I like how easy it is to access DOMO. It's easy to filter down to the exact metric you are looking for down to the date, metric and number amount. I also like how easy it is to export the file into a Microsoft Excel pdf. When I need to send me findings, I always need to attach the DOMO report which is easy to download.
cons: DOMO can be a bit confusing and overwhelming to use when you first look at it. But this is because of how detailed it takes you when you are searching for your data. Once you get the hang of it and know how to save your filters, it is a piece of cake to utilize and is very effective.

review_title: Nice to look at, not the easiest to navigate
author: Eleanor B.
pros: The data it puts out is fairly easy to digest, and you can make edits to the cards in real time. It's also easy to collaborate with colleagues on projects within Domo.
cons: The interface itself isn't the most intuitive. Trying to navigate to different boards/projects can feel a bit jumbled at times. It can also be confusing figuring out how to request to join a card/project if you don't know who the owner is, or having to contact them outside the platform since the notification system doesn't always seem to... notify people.

author: Yogesh M.
pros: It provided a good and easy to manipulate data visualization platform.
cons: Etl functions and heavier dashboards slowed the entire page down and was difficult to work with

description: My fast hard experience with Domo has been remarkable. The software is very standard.
author: Gideon B.
pros: Software is easy to learn and set up. It does not require training. Domo software provides reliable digital solutions that saves time and resources.
cons: The software has delivered according to expectations.

author: Olivia B.
pros: I like the numerous reports you can run. There are reports for each department and it is customizable which is great.
cons: It does not update in ream time. This is frustrating when we have deadlines and need accurate information.

description: Superb! Excellent! Top Notch, Good Visuals
author: Deborah M.
pros: Domo is the platform for us! It has all the capabilities we were looking for all in one.
cons: Onboarding was a little harder than we thought but well worth it!
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

author: Adrianna T.
pros: That it has multiple forms to showcase data and selections to create reports.
cons: It was simple in many functions and we only displayed it for day to day sales tracking.

review_title: Easy to Learn Data Visualization Tool
author: Liam S.
pros: I like the speed I can create new cards to better understand the data behind user complaints or potential areas to improve our system and customer experience. I also appreciate that it was easy to learn the software on the job with a limited background in data.
cons: I sometime wish I could create my own card template, and have had to export data to excel to create specific visualizations. Additionally, combining data sources can be clunky.

review_title: DOMO covers BI needs of my team, excited to see new updates and product development
author: Mariia K.
pros: As a person who mostly works with ETL and Data visualization I can say that cards and dashboards approach allows developer/analyst to visualize and analyze data, but business users will find it complicated. Working with various data sources, scheduling pipeline updates and reports, setting up alerts, and managing data lineage is easy.
cons: Writing and debugging SQL code in ETL pipelines is complicated and long process. Cards are not very responsive to resizing. DOMO has similar to Tableau LOD calculations, but they are not as flexible as DAX.

description: My experience is awesome, I worked in DOMO for almost 5+years and I love this tool
author: Gunjit K.
pros: Its a combination of everything Data extraction --> building ETL--> Dashboard Creation
cons: DOMO is a bit expensive, and the higher management must consider that when compared with other BI tools.

author: Benny F.
pros: Domo pulls data quickly from Salesforce, which is vital for our company. Salesforce reporting isn't excellent, but Domo gives us everything we need.
cons: It is not the easiest to navigate initially, but it is not difficult to figure out.

description: Solid product - fairly easy to use, good room for customization
author: Shivam P.
pros: Data visualization (graphs, tables, etc.). Domo pulls data from my organization's CRM system and other sources, making Domo a single place to view information.
cons: Updates made in a source take some time to flow through into Domo, it's not instantaneous.

author: Elian A.
pros: I can review my metrics to ensure that I am meeting my goals.
cons: I do not receive a second report at the end of the day only at the begging of the day.

review_title: A Powerful and Brilliant Analytics and Business Intelligence Tool.
description: It is now possible to make data-driven business decisions with Domo.Again,it is possible to uncover actionable data insights with Domo.
author: Ram D.
pros: With Domo,it is simple to create stunning data visualizations in real-time.It is simple to prepare and explore data with Domo.It is possible to analyze complex data with Domo.Again,it is simple to create customizable data reports and dashboards with Domo.
cons: Until now I'm yet to encounter any drawbacks with Domo.

review_title: Easy to learn and get value out of right away
description: Overall, the experience was positive. When I go into the office, I see Domo pages and charts all over the place. Many different teams are using Domo for their specific interests.
author: John W.
pros: To get started with Domo is a very smooth learning curve. You don't need to be an expert to start getting value right away. Both the technical side and the casual users were able to take advantage of Domo immediately.
cons: We had some small hiccups with performance that we needed help with. On occasion we had slower than expected response times from Domo support.

review_title: Domo is intuitive, modern, and helps your business monitor KPI's
description: Domo has become critical for managing our business and has provided us real time visibility into all areas.
author: Dylan C.
pros: I liked that you didn't have to have a developer background to effectively use the tool. Everything is laid out and simplified in a way that allows you to create visualizations in a user friendly way.
cons: There aren't many cons but some of the pre-built connectors don't work as intended and it's the responsibility of the vendor to fix.

description: I have personal experience with several of Domo's competitors as an analytics consultant. None of the Storage/ETL/BI use cases I can think of would I put domo in my top 3 options. I have found it to be subpar in data warehousing, query editing, and data visualization which from what I can tell is the majority of its functionalities.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: This product provides a web-based hub for your data and visuals that centralizes where your team focuses to interact with their data. The ETL tools provide an easy to use low/no code interface to build simple queries.
cons: Where Domo has removed complication (I assume to ease the use) they have dramatically limited this software capabilities in comparison to competitors. limitations include: - No ability to build visuals based on multiple tables - No ability to build filtering capabilities across tables - No ability to build relational models with existing tables - All "scripting" capabilities are glitchy and limited in their capabilities - Poor/nonexistent version control options - Compute resources are not brokered well and during high traffic times we can have dataflows run for up to 2 hours My worst experience with domo was with their customer service/integration team. One of their prebuilt integrations to a popular Web App, which domo is responsible for maintaining and servicing, has never functioned properly with our domo instance. The support case has been open for nearly a month and has just been escalated to someone that appears to know how to read the code I have been sending them to trouble shoot THEIR integration. They have promised resolution timelines twice and are now 2 weeks behind their initial promise.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: Domo makes it easy to create clear and interesting visualizations that empower users with accurate data to make effective business decisions. I also like that it easy to change chart types mid-stream if I should decide that I want to take a different approach. Lastly, Domo's functions/calculations capabilities make it easy to map data or display standard global metrics with disparate data.
cons: It would be nice if Domo provided more options for color coding summary data (text and/or numbers). I also appreciate more gauge options both in terms of functionality and colors.

review_title: Domo Is expensive but well worth the investment
description: I really like Domo it's hands down my favorite piece of software and also my favorite company to work with. It has solved countless reporting problems an saved thousands of hours spent repetitive reporting tasks for our team.
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It's incredibly powerful and allows any one (regardless of coding or data science background) the ability to create dynamic reports and complex data automations. The best part of Domo though is the customer support. When you call support you get a human right away and they are always ready to help regardless of how complicated your problem is or how much time it will take to solve. You never feel helpless with Domo, only empowered
cons: It costs a fortune and they have no entry level packages. they also have a habit of releasing awesome features that aren't included in the main package and that cost puts the platform well out of reach for most companies.
author_profession: Director of Sponsored Content & Audience Data

author: Morgan H.
pros: It's generally pretty easy to get data into Domo and the ETL options make it pretty easy to do any necessary data cleaning and transformation. The platform is user friendly and building visuals are straightforward, although they can be limited at times.
cons: Domo's support team definitely needs improvement - their response time is lacking and frequently ask for information that has already been provided in the initial ticket submission. It's also easy for the platform to become very disorganized - Domo could definitely benefit from implementing some data catalog functionality.

review_title: Domo Is a Reliable Business Intelligence Solution
author: Claudia B.
pros: This business intelligence solution has exceptional capabilities. I like how efficient Domo has been.
cons: Domo has been a flawless business intelligence solution. Domo is amazing to use.

review_title: Great with a dash of salt
description: Overall experience is good. Just need more training options for specific subjects. Update the developer site.
author: Patrick J.
pros: Very powerful and very flexible software. Lots of options to get data into Domo and to manipulate it.
cons: Training/Learning. I have to search all over the web to learn how to do things. When I work with Domo developers I'm told the training material is out-of-date and I should be doing something another way. (I appreciate the honesty of the developers, just not the out of date training material). Need some kind of tier training system. Not every user needs all the nitty-gritty details, but others like myself who have to work daily in domo need better understanding of the details.

author: Christian D.
pros: It compiles all necessary work and data in one place for management and other colleagues to plan accordingly for the day
cons: Length of time between updated data to refresh is pretty long

review_title: The things Domo can do are just astounding
description: We can see analytics we could never see before. We can understand our business in a way we never could before. We honestly don't know what we'd do without Domo at this point. It's fantastic.
author: Cole G.
pros: We were originally looking for dashboard software. Something like Geckoboard, but we wanted something more advanced. We were using multiple tools, and wanted to collect data from them. We then found Domo. Within days we had integrated our CRM database, Website Analytics, Invoicing database, and Order Processing database. This let us take a look at EVERYTHING. And I mean everything. Things like: What's the average time between receiving a lead and the rep calling them the first time? What's the average time between a customer ordering, and reordering? How many calls have my reps made today? (Updated every 15 minutes!) What landing pages on my website are receiving the most looks? How are my google analytic campaigns translating into converted customers?
cons: It's pricey, but worth it. The biggest thing was the last update they had screwed up some of my reports, and we're still trying to figure out what happened.
author_profession: Director Of Strategic Partnerships & Key Accounts
